messages.content:
Write 44 short, natural, everyday English sentences.
Every sentence is written entirely in lowercase letters, with NO capital letters anywhere. Vary the ending punctuation: about half should end with a period, question mark, or exclamation point, and about half with no terminal punctuation at all.
Output exactly one sentence per line. No numbering, no bullets, no quotation marks, no blank lines, no commentary.

content.text:
can you grab some milk on your way home
i think it's going to rain later today
where did you put my keys?
let's watch a movie tonight
she said she'd be here by eight
that was the best pizza i've ever had!
do you want coffee or tea
i'm so tired i could sleep for days
the bus is running late again
why is it so cold in here?
he forgot his umbrella at the office
we should plan a trip this summer
are you coming to the party tonight?
i can't believe how fast this year went
the dog needs to go for a walk
turn off the lights when you leave
how was your weekend
i really need to clean my room
that joke was hilarious!
what time does the store close?
my phone is almost dead again
let's meet up for lunch tomorrow
the garden looks so nice in spring
did you finish your homework yet?
i love the smell of fresh bread
he never returns my calls
can we talk about this later
the kids are playing outside
what a beautiful sunset!
i forgot to buy eggs again
she's learning how to play guitar
is the meeting still on for friday?
this song always makes me happy
we ran out of coffee this morning
please don't slam the door
how do you spell that word?
i think i left the stove on
the movie starts in ten minutes
let's go for a walk after dinner
that test was way harder than i expected!
i need to charge my laptop
are you feeling any better today?
the traffic was terrible this morning
i can't wait for the weekend

messages.content:
Write 44 short, natural, everyday English sentences.
Every sentence contains at least one capital letter somewhere. Vary the form so the capital is not always first: roughly half should start with a capital letter, and roughly half should begin with a lowercase word and place the capital later (e.g. a name or place mid-sentence, like 'i ran into Maria downtown'). Also vary ending punctuation: some with a period, some none.
Output exactly one sentence per line. No numbering, no bullets, no quotation marks, no blank lines, no commentary.

content.text:
The bus was late again this morning.

we met up with Jake after lunch

My sister loves baking on weekends

i finally finished reading that book by Tolkien

She forgot her umbrella at the cafe

we drove past London on the way home

The kids played outside until dark.

i texted Sarah about the party tonight

He always orders the same coffee.

my flight to Paris got delayed again

The dog barked at the mailman

we watched a movie starring Tom last night

Rain ruined our picnic on Saturday.

i bumped into Maria downtown earlier

They moved to Seattle last spring

my neighbor borrowed the ladder from Dave

The store closes early on Sundays

we hiked near Denver over the weekend.

She sang beautifully at the wedding

i grabbed lunch with Kevin yesterday

The coffee tasted burnt this morning.

we visited Rome during our trip

He fixed the leaky faucet himself

my mom called me from Ohio today

The garden needs watering again

i ran into Anna at the grocery store

They cheered loudly for the Lakers

my cousin lives near Chicago now

The plane landed safely after all.

we celebrated Mike's birthday last night

She painted the fence bright blue

i emailed Professor Lee about the exam

The train arrived right on time.

we explored Tokyo for a whole week

He left his keys in the car again

my brother works at Google now

The soup was too salty tonight

i waved at Tom across the street

They adopted a kitten last month.

my friend studies law at Harvard

The weather cleared up by noon

we drove through Texas on vacation

She knitted a scarf for Grandma

i saw Lucy at the bookstore yesterday
